meaning: The ability to keep doing a difficult physical activity for a long time.
example: Running a marathon requires a high level of physical endurance.

meaning: The physical or mental strength to continue an activity without getting tired.
example: Swimming is a great way to build up your stamina.

meaning: The process of drinking enough water to keep your body healthy and working well.
example: Proper hydration is very important when you exercise in hot weather.

meaning: Gentle exercises you do before a workout to prepare your muscles.
example: A ten-minute warm-up helps prevent injuries during heavy lifting.

meaning: Slow and easy exercises you do at the end of a workout to help your body relax.
example: Walking slowly on the treadmill is a good way to cool down.

meaning: Doing a single movement or exercise one time (often called a 'rep').
example: Try to complete ten repetitions of this exercise in each set.

meaning: A group of repetitions of an exercise done without stopping.
example: He completed three sets of fifteen push-ups.

meaning: A short bar with weights at each end, held in one hand for exercise.
example: She picked up a light dumbbell to exercise her arm muscles.

meaning: A long metal bar with weights on both ends, held with two hands.
example: He loaded weights onto the barbell before starting his squats.

meaning: An exercise machine with a moving belt that you can walk or run on in one place.
example: It was raining outside, so I ran for three miles on the treadmill.

meaning: Exercises that use only your own body weight to build strength and fitness.
example: Push-ups, pull-ups, and squats are common examples of calisthenics.

meaning: The time your body needs to rest and repair muscles after hard exercise.
example: Adequate sleep and good food are essential for muscle recovery.

meaning: An injury caused by twisting a joint, like an ankle or wrist, which causes pain and swelling.
example: She tripped during the race and suffered a painful ankle sprain.

meaning: The speed at which your heart beats, measured in beats per minute.
example: You can use a smartwatch to check your heart rate while running.

meaning: Exercises that strengthen the heart and lungs, like running or cycling.
example: I do thirty minutes of cardio three times a week to stay healthy.

meaning: The ability of joints and muscles to bend and move easily through their full range.
example: Yoga is excellent for improving your balance and flexibility.

meaning: Exercising too much or too hard without enough rest, leading to fatigue or injury.
example: Overtraining can make you feel tired and reduce your athletic performance.

meaning: An elastic band used in strength training to make exercises harder.
example: She wrapped the resistance band around her legs to do squats.

meaning: A person who watches and helps someone who is lifting weights to keep them safe.
example: Can you act as my spotter while I lift this heavy barbell?

meaning: A dangerous condition caused by losing too much water from the body.
example: Headache and dizziness are common signs of dehydration.

meaning: The process by which your body converts food and drink into energy.
example: Regular weight training can help speed up your metabolism.

meaning: A substance in food like meat, eggs, and beans that helps build and repair muscles.
example: Drinking a protein shake after a workout can help your muscles heal faster.

meaning: Energetic physical exercises done to music, designed to improve fitness.
example: My grandmother attends an aerobics class at the local community center.

meaning: The muscles around your stomach, back, and hips that keep your body stable.
example: Planks are one of the best exercises to strengthen your core.

meaning: Physical damage or harm done to a person's body during sports or exercise.
example: Stretching before you run can help reduce the risk of a muscle injury.

meaning: The ability to move quickly and change direction easily.
example: The soccer player showed great agility by dribbling past three defenders.

meaning: The ability to remain steady and not fall over while standing or moving.
example: Gymnastics helps children develop a good sense of balance.

meaning: The ability to use different parts of the body together smoothly and efficiently.
example: Playing tennis requires excellent hand-eye coordination.

meaning: The speed at which you run, walk, or move.
example: You need to set a steady pace at the start of the race so you do not get tired too quickly.

meaning: A single long step taken when running or walking.
example: The runner had a long, smooth stride that helped him cover distance quickly.

meaning: A person who is trained or skilled in exercises, sports, or games.
example: The Olympic athlete trained for eight hours every day.

meaning: Strong, fit, and active; physically good at sports.
example: He has a very athletic build because he plays basketball and runs regularly.

meaning: How well a person plays, runs, or does an activity.
example: The team's performance in the second half was much better than in the first.

meaning: A natural ability to do something well, without training.
example: She has a natural talent for gymnastics and learned the moves very quickly.

meaning: A particular way of doing an activity that requires skill or practice.
example: The coach helped him correct his swimming technique to move faster through the water.
